evidence_quotes.quote: not allowing addictive models, such as gambling, into child dominated spaces.
invalidate or destroy the product that I had previously bought.

paragraphs.text: Consumer protections are becoming increasingly vital in a late stage capital global society, one that is quietly becoming more and more dominated by corporations with substantial influence over laws, regulations and politics. Above all, I believe that as consumers we have a right to protect ourselves from corporate overreach. If I purchase a product and that product then later on becomes no longer profitable for the company I bought it from, in no version of this world should it be legal for that company to invalidate or destroy the product that I had previously bought. Ownership cannot be defined by companies. If buying isn't owning then it is theft. Additionally, I believe that for a corporation to offer a product and/or a service in the EU, that product has to comply with EU laws and regulation within reason. This includes not allowing addictive models, such as gambling, into child dominated spaces. There are already numerous people who have grown addicted to gambling during childhood due to the actions of Microsoft and Mojang. However the system within the EU meant to facilitate arbitration and legal action against legal entities outside of the EU have been found woefully lacking. A governments purpose is to help its people. Not just organize everything. Protecting consumers and the middle class from exploitation by elite groups is a must to maintain longterm stability.
